text: Since the 1998 Reformation, the Indonesian nation-state has gone from a monocultural attempt to enforce a unitary national identity, to what can be characterized as an atmosphere of more cultural diversity and religion competition. This has led to the flourishing of a myriad of competing ideas systems; liberal, moderate and fundamentalist. This paper seeks to elicit the constitutive elements of religion of the middle path of Islam in Indonesia. Using Indonesian Islamic learning institutions, namely the Islamic boarding school known as a pesantren, as a microcosm or miniature of Islam in Indonesia. As it has long been an institution for the propagation of Islam in Indonesia and has passed down knowledge, and tradition which could be categorized as representing the middle path of Islam — that of the Wali Songo with its character of propagation through peaceful means, assimilation and through Sufi traditions. Henceforth it may be inferred that the values of wasatiyyah of Islam in Indonesia has existed and still ongoing for centuries through its institutions, including the values of wasatiyyah through the media of *kitab kuning* and its implementation of values of tasamuh, tawasut and ta’adul has been shown to be carried out systematically. Thus, as the institutional infrastructure for representing Indonesian Islam, it is found that the Islamic Boarding school is able to negotiate between universal aspects of the religion and culture of the nation so as to be in line with societal demands; that as the best solution to defend against extremist ideologies and in order to reach the goal of achieving peace through mutual living of the diversity of cultures in Indonesia.
